methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
MineSense Technologies, a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Jeff More, has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 21 Climate & Energy peers—including 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms such as Ascend Elements, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems—though a sufficiently tight peer set for median-based valuation proved unavailable.

Stage-progression methodology was the primary applicable approach. Using the $100M+ total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, the analysis implies a valuation of $120M. This method reflects MineSense's progression within the Growth stage and baseline investor confidence anchored in capital deployed.

The triangulated valuation range of $102M to $138M, centered at $120M median, reflects limited methodological depth. The peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and same-tier comparables; while the broader Climate & Energy sector contains 21 peers, the narrow filters (stage, valuation tier, and operational similarity) could not generate three or more datapoints. The analysis therefore rests on stage-progression alone. Key uncertainties include relative unit economics and revenue traction versus the identified peer set, as well as capital efficiency metrics that would sharpen peer-based anchoring. Validation would benefit from additional private market transactions in the narrowly-defined frontier energy subsector.
